channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: The US has 10 days — 43 replies — 172.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
The US has 10 days

Open thread • Replies: 43 • Updated: 2026-03-05 09:08 UTC

OP

The US has 10 days to clear the strait of Hormuz or global markets will crash (not a hyperbole)

At first, markets brushed off the conflict as another 12 day war. However, realization is setting in that Iran is actually capable of deterring passage through the Hormuz strait by 90%.

The problem is that every day that goes on is the equivalent of shutting off 10+ billion barrels of oil every single day. As storage becomes full, producers are forced to shut in production, which takes 2 weeks to restart. Thankfully, most of the world is fine for 1-2 weeks without gulf oil. After that, problems begin.

Oil will reprice a geopolitical premium and $100 is LOW for the magnitude of production shutdown.

What happens: bond yields will roar to new highs as a huge wave of inflation begins. All rate cut probabilities get removed, and you will see the traditional end of the business cycle, high oil prices will turn growth into recession for most economies. None of this is priced in equities. And the longer it goes on, the higher likelihood of it happening.

How to end it? Trump has fallen for a Trump. There is no way to clear irans fast mountain ranges littered with AD and missile silos, thus tankers will keep getting shot in the Hormuz strait. The only way is to put troops on the ground.

Don’t really care if you think I’m a permabear or a doomer, I’m just giving you a warning that for whatever reason, equities don’t want to price in. They don’t understand that this is an existential war for Iran, it’s not some “geopolitical dip”.

Pic is Asian jet fuel prices

Early replies (first 3)

61938343
>>61938341
Slurp XOP? Wut do

61938344
So should I sell my TLT?

61938345
>>61938341
>I’m just giving you a warning that for whatever reason, equities don’t want to price in
Trump spent all of last year insider trading and TACOing so he's not trustworthy anymore

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61938411
>>61938402
Literally happened yesterday.

61938425
fucking retard trump, how can an administration be so fucking stupid, didn't this netanyahu who seems way smarter than trump see this coming, is the world run by literal dolts?

61938428
>>61938402

Nah, it’s simply too close to Iran. Putting anything in the sea or air 25 miles from silos is just asking to get blown up

Only way is with ground troops stationed on the coasts

61938435
>>61938425
We has exactly one administration left to NOT do something like this and turn it around by investing in industry instead of another war in the middle east.
Well, time to accept an entire generation of diminishing wages and returns on capital. Probably a currency crisis by the end of it. Fucking hell

61938437
>>61938435
>>61938425
We had*

61938445
>>61938425
I'm beginning to think that one of the underlying objectives was to tank the economies of EU, India and asia as whole. One way to onshore manufacturing and heavy industry is to be one of the few places with relatively cheap energy and the US is surplus producer of oil. I think those WW3 scitzos might have been onto something.

61938452
>>61938445
the us can't supply the world with oil, not at the scale the world needs, the venezuela plants will need a couple of months to get back to steady levels and most of the world's refineries can't even process it, also the us also buys from the middle east since it's so spread out and big, it doesn't have like a standardized oil policy despite being a net exporter, it's a clusterfuck of fuckups, the only way this war ends is if the israelis vote netanyahu out, or if a coup happens in israel, or if he resigns, this could get very ugly very fast

61938454
>>61938341
what part of you VILL have nothing you failed to understand?

61938457
>>61938452
That’s what he is saying. It would be the best place to invest in after this if it works out the way he’s explained it

61938472
>>61938457
but even if the us exports surge, they still can't meet the world's oil demands for things like daily vehicle use, how is this supposed to solve that problem, or house heating, or electricity production, you can't recalibrate an economy to stop using oil in 2 weeks, if this was the plan, trump and netanyahu would have held off for a few months till venezuela's plants were running again, there would have been some alternative

61938487
>>61938472
Let me clarify: it’s not about theUS supplying oil to the world. It’s about the US being the best place to expand production because everywhere else has unreliable or energy.

61938490
10 days, two more weeks?

61938494
>>61938472
>>61938487
unreliable or expensive access to energy*

61938502
>>61938341
hormuz doesn't matter at all for US. has 0 influence on us. the war will have some impact but it doesn't really matter, the most impactful part of all this is shelling out free money to israel which lowers trust in the market. as well gay oil and energy companies taking advantage of the already strapped consumer yet again
instead of domestic energy being invested in jews need that

61938506
>>61938487
that logic still makes no sense since if it works, you are bankrupting the world, who will buy your goods if they don't have energy to run their houses or drive their cars, energy is a basic need, after food

61938510
>>61938341
irrelevant
i sleep

61938517
>>61938506
A normal person isn’t the investor here lmao. You think billionaires, multinationals, and global investment firms will be struggling to eat?

61938518
Retard here, so what should I put my money into long term?

61938521
>>61938506
>>61938517
It won’t be production of everyday goods m8

61938522
>>61938517
no investor profits in a scenario where peasants don't have access to basic needs, this is a retarded argument

61938528
>>61938341
This crisis will be resolved in 5 days 5

>>61938411
>>61938425
>>61938428
There is no reason to be this upset. Jews and Sauds and US and shipping companies are cutting a deal with insurance and logistics guarantees.

61938532
>>61938522
Okay what do you think will be produced here and how poor do you think the whole world will get? You’re being hyperbolic.

61938534
>>61938506
Logic? Introducing a financial tactics from a hundred years ago (tariffs) to somehow try to force companies to produce shit at higher cost in you country and thus fucking everyone over, where's the logic in that? We have entered the Idiocracy timeline, abandon all hopes of competency and reason.

61938536
>>61938528
yes they can cut all the deals they want, it doesn't guarantee tankers won't be bombed, remember that the red sea is also being chocked by the houthis

61938544
>>61938532
what do you mean, what will be produced, the rest of the world relies on propane gas and kerosene for cooking, not like in the us where electricity is cheap enough for induction cookers, there's also the issue of jet fuel for transportation, there's also electricity generation that relies on diesel, vehicle fuel, etc if this is not available for one week, what do you think will happen to the rest of the world while they wait for the us to ship them consumer electronics and other non essentials, wtf are you talking about

61938547
>>61938544
You’re obviously confused and I no longer want to explain shit. Good day

61938558
>>61938536
Anon shipping doesn't stop. It can be delayed but it will not stop. These guys are always under threat, what has changed since the days of black beard? Irgc, hezbollah and the various ragtag sunni and shia militias funded by iran and hamas will extremely soon lack enough firepower to seriously damage these big ships. The guy who said they're about down to lawn mower engines is right. It actually makes me furious we lived in global fear for 50 years because of these psychos and nobody had the political will to go in and finally kill the main bad guy after all the expensive boondoggles chasing their shadow.

61938569
>>61938425
Bibi doesn't care, this was all his doing anyway. The goal is to remove the final major opposition that Israel has in the middle-east and they can do whatever they want because USA will bail their asses both militarily and economically if anything were to happen.

61938578
>>61938569
What is israel's next goal after iran? Dont say greater israel, that is retarded.

61938587
>>61938341
Sure, but all of this can end in 1 message.
> Hormuz Strait open again
Will it come at some point?

item.title: Anyone else getting the itch? — 100 replies — 57.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Anyone else getting the itch?

Open thread • Replies: 100 • Updated: 2026-03-05 07:26 UTC

OP

The big boys'll go back up, that's guaranteed money. But some others will 10x, 100x, 1000x. I'm feelin' like it's SHIB's turn.

Early replies (first 3)

61900851
>>61899833
Checked

61900872
>>61899833
I'll just keep buying small amounts of it for the rest of my life, and see what happens.

61900948
>>61899833
i believe in shib

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61923250
>>61922734
When toad moons YOU'RE going to seethe, when toad moons YOU'RE going to rope!

61923260
>>61899833
I am in anon, people are too stupid to skip this one.
And some Doge in case the elom makes a comeback.

61924787
>>61922734
See
>>61922014

61926036
>>61899833
This thread smells like curry and shit.

Also, shibaggies holding their shibags to zero.

61926122
>>61926036
>This thread smells like curry and shit.
So like most threads on /biz ?

>>61926036
>Also, shibaggies holding their shibags to zero.
Sure buddy. The number 1 meme on eth is going to zero goy!

61927655
>>61926122
>The number 1 meme on eth is going to zero goy!
i'm genuinely considering dumping the rest of what i hold because of this post

61927803
>>61927655
Checked dump it

61927812
>>61899833
you will be lucky to get a 3x

61927816
>>61927812
Shib is ez 10x bear case 33x bull case

61927892
>>61927816
Never happening

61928058
>>61927892
>Shib will never go to a place it’s been before because it just won’t.
You sound tarded bro

61928094
>>61928058
It only went there because of insane hype and FOMO. It will never happen again unless Elon makes it the official currency of X or something

61928421
>>61927655
Please do fud chud. I like cheapies.
>>61927812
>>61928094
He didn't buy cheap before the etf and clarity act passed.
>NGMI!

61929970
> shib niggers in this thread ignoring the most obvious 1000-3000x token right in front of them

61931432
>>61929970
Its no use anon. These people like being poor. Sit back and hodl and wait for the fireworks.

61931717
Shib is positive returns for evm daily trade bots. Over 60% positive returns. So far they’re all trading at above 50% positive returns for bot trading. What ever happened to shiba sex? Still a general.

61931779
This thread makes me so happy, I thought all my Shibros had abandoned /biz/ forever...we're back in full force!

61932425
While retards in the chat slurping a shitcoin that’s already over 5 years olde, the smart money is dropping at least .2 eff on toad, just sitting back watching the drama unfold

61934414
50x

61934942
it begins...

61935122
Toads invade shib threads because its a zero volume zero interest and zero attention, its been a shit coin since early 2023. 1.9k holders that want to kys. Lol, lmao even.

61935440
>>61931779
SHIB blessing thread anon still here, i'm still here waiting for the time and it's going to happen

61936417
>>61935122
No u. Toad holders are going to make it.

61936442
my itch is on cheese, already bottomed only upwards from here

61936453
>>61936442
No fuck off with that jeet scam. Toad is not a jeet scam it's an Israeli/US government scam.

61937292
>>61899833
Luna never got a crack last time

61937454
>>61899833
I own $10,000 worth of Shib and that pile of shit isn't going anywhere.
Once is goes 25% up I'm dumping it asap.

61937481
>>61937454
>isn't going
>once is goes
?

61937662
>>61937454
Smart. Grab a moonbag of toadkiller and prepare for the biggest dopamine rush of your life

61938369
>>61937454
I suggest not selling your entire bag because then you'll be one of those people saying they want to rope when it m00ns

item.content:encoded:
13,000 LINK

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-03-05 09:04 UTC

OP

Go on without me bros. I can't get to 100k in even ten years :(

Early replies (first 3)

61937328
>he had 700,000 bucks in 2021 at the top and didn't sell

61937341
>>61937328
Thanks peeler. I started buying in 3 years ago and have DCA's ever since

61937376
>me with my lil stack of 2000 LINK :)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61937380
>>61937376
Based doge

61937447
>>61937311
>>61937341
>>61937380
ah it's my favorite kind of stinkie
the low 5 figure amount who didn't sell any appreciable amount
maybe he's staking a bit
but all around: he's a loser
guess what stinkbag: ur never gonna win
the day of salvation is not coming

61937451
>>61937447
>the low 5 figure amount who didn't sell
Why would I sell something that I'm barely in the green on after three years? The capital gains tax would have killed me
>maybe he's staking a bit
I wish
>guess what stinkbag: ur never gonna win
How's your porn identity looking? Into furry tranny yet?

61938304
>Can't get to 100k
I don't know why you would want to but soon you will be able to very easily if you just really feel like draining your savings to findom serve sirgay

61938313
STINKY

61938497
money talks

61938577
>>61937311
You already have a make it stack you'll be fine in 10 years.

item.title: Top of the dead cat bounce? — 9 replies — 36.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Top of the dead cat bounce?

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-03-05 09:05 UTC

OP

We just got denied at 73.8K, the top of March 2024's first selloff. This will be a VERY IDEAL time to short, if we really drop and didn't break out for at least 3 days from that line.

Early replies (first 3)

61938413
>>61938409
for newfags, who don't understand what i mean, there's always 1 event that follows a dead cat bounce at the end of every bullrun on every market

61938418
>>61938409
>>61938413
Experienced anon. Listen to him

61938423
no m8 it's all bs everything is it's just some richfags screwing with everyone because they're spiritually dead and can't enjoy life any other way. the richfags will pump and dump for the lols regardless of the chart patterns

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61938424
>>61938409
$90k is the dead cat bounce, thats when you go full short

61938430
>>61938418
Thanks. Im just trying to help newerfags in playing the game a bit better, like the older fags who helped me back in 2018

>>61938423
>just some richfags screwing with everyone
Yea no shit everyone knows about it. This ain't /pol/ m8. Here, you either get fucked or join the richfags. And if you choose to get fucked, then stop dragging clueless newfags to your lower standard of life

61938434
>>61938424
I did short on 90K (well, actually i shorted a bit late, around 84K, just to be safe). But in my last thread, which is months ago, someone asked when will be the second best time to short, for those who didnt short aroun 90K.

That time has come, i think, if we didnt break out from 74K

61938446
>>61938424
I’d agree with you if there weren’t a fast moving military campaign in the middle east with consequences for the entire global economy.

61938481
>>61938446
Exactly what i mean. Plus in my opinion, that 90K drop started above my blue line. So in my pov, thats the start of bear market, not the top of the dead cat. Hell we wont even know if this rejection at 73.8K really is dead cat bounce's top. We need to wait for at least till sunday EST.

So if y'all wanna short now, please use a safe amount of margin, and a reasonable stop loss

61938582
I saw this prediction from IvanOnTech on youtube, he put out a new video today. Seems similar to what you're saying a bit. He was mostly right about the last bull run but I didn't listen to him and instead listened to some other people that ended up being wrong about everything so I missed most of it.

item.title: Guys.. — 19 replies — 24.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Guys..

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-03-05 08:24 UTC

OP

NO ONE WILL hire me
How do I actually make money without getting a physical job? I know it is possible because other people are doing it:

Early replies (first 3)

61934866
>>61934860
have you tried mcdonalds?

61934874
>>61934866
I did. I even got to the interview stage. Then they told me they werent actually hiring. I tried pizza hut too btw.

61934892
>>61934874
Why don't you want to become a Pizza Hut Talent™? I'm sure if you work hard and join the top 0.01% of all legacy humans, they will one day offer a pay-to-work internship through some government scheme.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61934898
>>61934860
I am in the same boat bro. I quit at the post office and now I am just drifting. Nobody will hire me. Best buy? No. Home depot? No. chick filet? No. Mobile phone repair? No.

61934922
>>61934898
fuck

61934926
If you do end up needing something in a pinch see if ULINE has anything than can tide you over

61934928
>>61934926
None in my area, thanks for the offer though
I suppose I should clarify what also hurts my chances of getting a job is I dont have a car. And i like in a small town. So that really fucking sucks

61934934
>>61934860
Work as a caregiver in home health. No cert needed. You'll wipe old people ass and are basically paid to be a boomers friend for a few days a week.

61934937
>>61934934
I will not do that.

61934941
>>61934937

You're going to fondle the balls that sold you out.

61934987
>>61934928

61935054
>>61934860
Violence against the state is ALWAYS hiring.

61935820
>>61934922
What do we do bros? I am very close to asking my mom for a loan.

61936087
>>61934860
Guess it's time to light some rich neighborhoods on fire then isn't it?

61936108
>>61935054
Against? Don’t you mean for?

61936118
>>61934898
bruh, you quit the post office. are you fucking retarded?
I'd kill for an easy cruise control kinda job

61936170
>>61934860
It is doable but, you will need to purchase assets as if you do not have these constraints and interact with them as if you have all the time in the world to minimize micromanaging yourself into underperformance, a method if you are a complete beginner is 1) find a asset you are interested in method of finding it does not matter , 2) analyze previous 10ks ( just get ai to help you if you are new but this is the part that will need constant education) and the DEF 14A (proxy statements) to get a rundown of what it is that the company does, you want to hold an asset that actually know roughly what it does 3) read reports and analysis from investment banks, financial news sites, investment forums (such as value investor club) and other forums look for reasons that the asset may be good and be aware of positive or negative biases, 4) combine 2 and 3 through analysis of their financial position as well as their actual business operation with what Conesus is of the asset and see if the price of the asset is and if it reflects this correctly. then buy accordingly

61936186
>physical job
I assume you mean physical labor, like construction or warehousing.
I was in this position a few months ago. Starbucks had a real human being (the manager of the local store) call me back within a few days to schedule an interview. Before this, I was getting teased by applying to fake datamining job postings and cucked by AI filters.
I'm making $16/hr and it keeps me afloat from month to month. Better than $0/hr and starving I guess.

61938466
>>61936118
Please. Please work at the post office. There is no reason at all whatsoever the term going postal exist. (Did you knoe that most of the the real work at the post office is done by part time employees who make 1/4 what regular welfare queen postal regulars make?) T. 3 year veteran

item.title: Blockchain Pill says $ICP will reach $80 — 14 replies — 20.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Blockchain Pill says $ICP will reach $80

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-03-05 08:29 UTC

OP

During the bull run of 2024, ICP reached $19, and that was before Caffeine.aim, before the Pakistan government and HBAR partnership. Some notable partners are Maestro, UN and Switzerland.
Ignore the clowns who judge the price because of manipulation at launch. Crypto is not like stocks, being -99% don't mean shit. Look at Z Cash chart. Hell even Eth was down 96% in 2018. None of that shit matters, the most important thing is that Dfinity, the organization behind ICP is fully committed and the technology is far more advanced than anything else in blockchain.

Early replies (first 3)

61935478
Would someone rich really be making youtube videos like an indentured instagram model?

61935482
Does anyone use it for anything other than inferior annoying knockoffs of mature efficient technologies.

61935484
>>61935478
Why is Asmongold streaming then? He's a billionaire.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61935490
>>61935484
CIA asset

61935494
>>61935490
So every streamer millionaire like Speed, Kai, XQC, Adin Ross.. They're all just CIA?

61935505
>>61935468
It's the only cryptocurrency I own.

61935520
>>61935494
The point here doesn't apply since their wealth comes from their streaming.

61935522
>>61935494
Not necessarily CIA but most are night media assets which belong to the Jews.

61935591
>>61935468
wow a paid romanian gypsy shill is promoting his free coins.

this gypsy is a known nft scammer. look up internet humans/ic kitties/puppies. not the best representative you want to have for your ghostchain.

61935624
>>61935591
>this gypsy is a known
He is whiter then you Muhammad.

61938278
>>61935468
Only 3 things on icp are home runs thatll do multiples more than icp as it moons, Dvinity, McDoms, and Dead.

Everything else will underperform as icp moons

61938281
>>61938278
Some charts to prove my point

61938287
>>61938278
No dead chart yet since it came out like 12 hours ago and icptokens is slow, but Passion is behind it

61938493
>>61935468
>pakistan government
worth shit, 3rd world country partnerships including dubai are worth nothing
remember LTO and their EU partnerships with afghanistan and a bunch of african countries

item.title: Day 1 of snailposting every day until JASMY is at $1 — 13 replies — 17.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Day 1 of snailposting every day until JASMY is at $1

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-03-05 08:24 UTC

OP

Day 1 of snailposting every day until JASMY is at $1

Early replies (first 3)

61934693
jasmy

61935114
>>61934344
10m jasmy daimyo here
2 words
Apple partnership
I've been stacking since 2023

61935154
based

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61935550
ohh jasmy

61935609
>>61935597
uhhhhhh what is this

61935646
>>61935609
>Jasmy now has a compliant domestic payment rail through its collaboration with Aplix Corporation, which was licensed by Japan’s Financial Services Agency to issue electronic money. This doesn’t confirm that JPYD is launched or that it is a bank-backed stablecoin, but it establishes the legal infrastructure needed to build and deploy regulated digital payments (wallets, merchant payments, local government programs, etc.) that could later integrate blockchain settlement. In short: Jasmy gains regulatory groundwork for real payment infrastructure in Japan, but the exact structure of JPYD is still unknown.

61935684
>>61935646
sounds interesting especially considering mcap is low rn
would this actually go to the token though? I thought the token was supposed to be for iot? I don't even remember anymore it's been years since I sold.

61936773
JASMY

61937984
>>61935114
>>61935684
good questions. Is apple buying jasms or not?

61938015
>>61935114
AI chatbots tell me that big companies are using Jasmy for "data lockers"... where is the evidence they are buying and pumping price?

61938024
(You) vill buy ze top

61938468
gm

item.title: Reminder even 100 million NW is not generational wealth worthy — 78 replies — 16.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Reminder even 100 million NW is not generational wealth worthy

Open thread • Replies: 78 • Updated: 2026-03-05 04:21 UTC

OP

Reminder even 100 million NW is not generational wealth worthy

Early replies (first 3)

61931291
This is just a coon who doesn't understand investments and compounding returns.

61931302
>>61931288
Probably bought a bunch of houses and cars that he couldn't afford the maintenance and taxes on

61931311
>>61931302
>>61931291
>>61931288
He's right though

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61933005
>>61931288
Why are you faggots obsessed with "generational" wealth? Even if you raise your children perfectly, it is only a matter of a few generations down the line before they become decadent and lose your fortune. At most just provide them a beater car, a starter house, and pay for their education.

61933411
>>61931288
they could give him 100 billion and he'd still go bankrupt
funny how you only see this with blacks, everyone else seems to have little issue managing money

61934349
>>61933005
>just let all the other groups that hate you have wealth and status bro

61934356
>>61931967
you can't read?

61934424
>>61933005
Black people love catchy sayings like "generational wealth", especially TV blacks. They get any kind of money and recognition and then they want to larp as Malcom X for some reason, even if they still do nigga shit like having baby mommas and getting arrested.

61934468
>>61932022

Rookies also go through a league mandated financial education class to help prevent the bankruptcies. Just really hard to get someone in the USA to understand temporality

61934564
>>61932553
Theres 8 billion niggas on earth
There are probably 10 million boomers worth over 1 million in california alone because of their houses they bought in the 90s at a quarter of the current valuation

61934569
>>61931288
But a Bieber BBC BJ is priceless

61934572
>>61934349
the shitmigrants around me definitely dont have generational wealth,,, theyre more of a nuisance .....

61934605
aren't there like black wealth managers or family offices for these retards in some place like atlanta, it would be a good way to get rich

61934641
>>61931311
oh man only $12 million a year, how do these poor oppressed black men survive on so little? with such tiny paychecks you won't even have any money left to invest after paying your bills

61934654
Most people don't know how to adjust after living like this. He's getting paid 20M a year, and he isn't going to be paid 20M a year for long. Even if he invests it well, he still isn't going to go back to 20M a year.

61934927
>>61931288
>niggerball player is bad with money, can't survive on a measly $100 million
This is why white people need to pay reparations. There are millions of niggers just like this in this country living hand to mouth because of racism. How is little Jayquerius expected to afford his malt liquor and hennessy in these conditions?

61937100
>>61934424
But here it is mostly white men who want to give the ideal trad upbringing to their children and make it possible several generations forward without relying on the evil pozzed world too much and for that they need a gajillion dollars.
The trvthpill here is that it is a game of chance. Every generation is a roll of the dice. Just do your best to raise your own family and don't try to plan 10 generations ahead.

61937293
>>61931302
>>61931362
Yup. To blow that much money they:

>Pay off debts of vaguely related friends and family
>Buy friends and family cars and other bling
>Stupidly big properties
>Loads of super cars
>Take an entourage to da club
>Hookers and blow

It's not to say $12M a year isn't a lot of money. But supporting a stupidly large lifestyle for 20 people going all out is only barely feasible for that.

If you just lived with your family and had a nice property or three and went to a nice wine store for your Dom Perignon and took yourself and the missus out to three star Michelin restaurants and flew first/business class and paid for housekeepers and gardeners and an assistant and bought a Patek for yourself and the lady once a year you totally could.

61937296
>>61931288
You have to be a genuine dumbass to be able to spend that amount of money

61937513
>>61931288
he deserves permanent debt

61937521
That is what happens when you don't live frugally and spend it all. All he had to do was live in a million dollar shit box in the nice neighborhood, go on an occasional vacation and send his kids through college. He would have spent only 5 million at most in his lifetime. Maybe 10 million if he had a bunch of emergency expenses. 10 generations of wealth right there not counting for inflation.

61937541
>>61937521
a) Darkies can't even plan ten minutes ahead, let alone twenty years.
b) I believe what >>61932636 said where sportsball leagues intentionally encourage players to be lavish and short-term in focus as a means of control; a player who's financially secure is in a position to dictate terms to the team/league. A player who's one missed paycheque away from insolvency isn't.

61937542
>>61937100
I see, you're responding to OPs comment, not example.
The real answer is because his comment was a shitpost and the real purpose of this thread is to laugh at low IQ pro athletes, I don't think this board is actually obsessed with generational wealth. A lot of guys here just like to imagine what ifs, like what if they ever had a family and were the monopoly man.

61937562
>>61933005
It's the illusion of stability. It especially makes sense for black people. White sons from the top quintile are like 80% likely to stay in in the top quintile. Black sons are only 20% likely, they almost always end up less successful than their parents. So, it makes sense for those parents to want to shore up their chances, since the system seems stacked against them.
But it really is a betrayal of the American ethos. Outcomes for white boys should be more like those of black boys, as an equal share of each quintile ending up in each quintile would be indicative of a true meritocracy. Most people don't seem to understand that, when they say that they want generational wealth, they're saying they want aristocracy.

61937567
>>61934605
It's just a coincidence that there don't seem to be enough of them.

61937570
>>61937542
Those two things aren't mutually exclusive and both are actually helpful if the true emotional goal is coping without actually changing anything about their own lives. Mindless fantasizing and punching down on perceived inferiors, perfect ingredients for a Copium and Tonic.

61937571
>>61937567
this is like saying if we built more libraries then these people would become more literate

61937575
>>61937521
If you had 55mill(post taxes) and literally lost all of it to the point of not even having a property you have to have had maximum idiotic decision making.
Maximized credits
Gifting money that you aren't getting back
Buying multiple depreciating resources
Extremely expensive alcohol and food every day
Paying double for jewellry worksmanship over material for bling
Hookers and blow maxxing every day

That said nigga probably lived all he had to live already

61937720
>>61937575
Hookers? He would have had fans who would have sex with him for free. Marry one that would be reliable for taking care of the home if not already married like most athletes do.

61937745
>>61932058
>Billie eyelash
What's wrong with her finances?

61937749
>>61937720
Kinda jelly though I only have a bit over 1m after full liquidation I know I can live tightly indefinitely with that but indefinitely won't last through a bad market crash with that but with about double I don't see how that could happen

61937766
>>61937571
Yes.
But also, it's just a coincidence.

61938098
>>61937766
how, because jews are the majority? or because they don't want their wealth managed by outsiders, considering that people they look up to like lebron and michael jordan obviously have their wealth maanged.

item.title: S&P500 PE ratio — 14 replies — 14.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
S&P500 PE ratio

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-03-05 08:10 UTC

OP

A return to mean would require a 60% drop.
Corrections often overcorrect. So... let's say 80% drop.

Early replies (first 3)

61934160
NOOOOO your historical record breaking profitable companies can't be highly valued because... they just CAN'T ok??!?

61936674
>>61934133
Explain in 33 words why? Why should it return to the 1880-1980 mean?

61936687
it can't return to the mean because that would require the us dollar supply to return to the mean. Its mathematically impossible now.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61936695
>>61934133
why dont we make it a clean 100%?

61936698
>>61936695
This is not a %, it can go beyond 100.

61936713
>>61934133
Everybody knows that fiat is dying, is the music chairs game in reverse.

61936727
>>61936687
no
you are a retard or a bot
you did not consider that both P and E are denominated in $

61936746
>>61936727
Did you know that the average PE ratio during previous centuries was in the single digits? Why didn't 1800-1900 mean revert back to 1700 levels? Why should 2000 mean revert back to 1900 levels?

61936764
>>61934133
>drop
>in the era of even faker and gayer money printer shenanigans

61936768
>>61936746
What about this? S&P500 vs US M2 supply

61936776
Or when comparing S&P500 total return to bond market total return

61936777
>>61934133
looks like it's going to 100 first

61936821
>>61936768
M2 has nothing to do with PE ratio.
Do global total stock market instead of SP500.
America may just be overvalued still (or maybe not).

61938431
>>61936821
M2 expansion (presumably not stopping any time soon) has to do with why valuations are going up regardless of PE ratio
